macro_segments.title: Establishing the Halacha on Whose Testimony About Blemishes is Believed

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman's Ruling Following Rabbi Shimon ben Gamliel
Rava's Ruling Following Rabbi Yosi and the Limits on Family Members
Challenge to Rava's Position: The Case of Owners Standing Outside
Resolution: We Do Not Suspect the Shepherd When Owners Are Absent
Final Halacha: Following Rabbi Shimon ben Gamliel, with the Spouse Exception
Digression on Kashrut Areas: Why Bechor is Different from Normal Issur V'Heter

macro_segments.title: The Logical Problem of Suspecting Kohanim While Accepting Them as Judges

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Challenge: If Kohanim Are Suspect on Bechor, How Can They Be Judges?
The Apparent Contradiction with Rabbi Meir's Rule About General Suspicion
The Answer: Distinction Between Class Suspicion and Individual Evidence
Illustration: When Do We Fully Disqualify Someone for Everything?

macro_segments.title: The Question of Second-Hand Testimony in Bechor Cases

micro_segments.title: The Raised Question: Is Second-Hand Testimony Permissible for Bechor?
Rav Assi and Rav Ashi Dispute: Forbidden vs. Permissible
The Baraita on Hearsay: Only for Cases Like the Agunah
The Reasoning: Women's Testimony Works So Hearsay Should Too
Final Ruling: Hearsay is Accepted for Bechor Testimony

macro_segments.title: The Case Where the Kohen Discloses the Bechor Status Himself

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar's Case: Animal Unknown as Bechor, Kohen Reveals and Claims Blemish
The Principle: The Same Mouth That Forbids Also Permits
Comparison to the Divorced Woman: Self-Disclosure Without Prior Knowledge
The Crucial Distinction: The Kohen Must Admit It Is a Bechor
Psychological Insight: The Kohen Won't Eat Kodashim Outside the Mikdash
The Challenge: If Forced to Admit, Why Believe Him About How It Got the Blemish?
Resolution: A Suspicious Blemish Suggests Honesty, Not Fabrication

macro_segments.title: The Story of Rabbi Tzadok, Rabbi Yehoshua, and Rabban Gamliel's Authority

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Tzadok's Bechor Gets a Blemish Accidentally; Questions Rabbi Yehoshua
Rabbi Yehoshua's First Answer: Yes, We Trust Distinguished Kohanim (Chaveirim)
Rabban Gamliel's Opposite View: No Distinction Between Chaveirim and Am Ha'aretz
Public Confrontation: Rabban Gamliel Humiliates Rabbi Yehoshua in the Study Hall
Rabbi Yehoshua's Inability to Deny: Both Are Alive and Present
The People's Reaction: Silencing Rabban Gamliel Over the Mistreatment
Deeper Reading: Power Dynamics Between Rabbinic Authority and Kohanim

macro_segments.title: What the Kohen Is and Is Not Believed About Regarding Blemishes

micro_segments.title: The Kohen Is Believed That a Blemish Is Genuine and Valid
The Kohen Is Also Believed That He Received It from an Israelite With the Blemish
The Principle: Anything That Will Be Uncovered People Don't Lie About
The Distinction: The Kohen Won't Eat Invalid Korbanos Outside the Mikdash
Why the Kohen Is Not Believed About How the Blemish Occurred
The Messenger Who Buys Ma'aser: When Checkability Matters and When It Doesn't
The Fear Factor: Specific Demands Create Credibility More Than General Statements
Remaining Question: How Do We Believe Kohanim on Borderline Blemishes?
